title: New Moon, Full Moon: A Monthly Ritual
subtitle: A simple two-part ritual for marking the lunar cycle as a personal check-in practice
excerpt: A two-part monthly ritual using the new moon and full moon as natural check-in points. The new moon practice sets an intention for the month ahead. The full moon practice reviews what has been and releases what is ready to go. No equipment required beyond a candle and a notebook.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. Lunar cycle rituals appear across many folk and indigenous traditions worldwide. The practice here draws on the general new moon / full moon structure common to many contemporary folk-spiritual practices, without claiming a specific tradition. The five-part ritual structure draws on the ceremony shape from MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025).

content.text: A two-part monthly ritual using the new moon and full moon as natural check-in points. Each takes five minutes. The new moon practice sets one intention for the month. The full moon practice reviews what has been and clears what is ready to go.

Light a candle on the evening of the new moon, or within a day of it. Have a notebook open. Write one thing you want to bring more of into the month ahead. One thing. Make it specific enough to recognise when it arrives.
I am ready to release what is standing between me and a good month. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to allow what I have written to come toward me this month. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Sit for two minutes with the candle and the written intention. Read the intention back once. Put the notebook somewhere you will see it.
This month is mine to shape.
Blow out the candle. The new moon practice is done.

On the evening of the full moon, light the same candle. Read back what you wrote at the new moon. Note what came toward it and what didn't, without judgement. Use the same structure: release what the month brought that you are ready to let go, allow what you want to carry forward. Blow out the candle.

Written for homemade.education. Marking the new moon and full moon as check-in points appears in many folk traditions worldwide. This practice uses the general new moon and full moon structure common to contemporary folk-spiritual practice, without claiming one specific tradition. The release-and-allow structure comes from MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025).
